methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials is a Series D climate and energy company founded by J. B. Straubel that has raised $2.3B+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $6.0B. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 private peers in the same sector and stage proximity, including KoBold Metals ($3.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M), plus one traded public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier from the last post-money of $6.0B, implying a forward valuation of $9.0B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yielded a median of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B. The peer median substantially discounts the current valuation, reflecting earlier stages and potentially divergent risk profiles within the climate-tech cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a range of $850M to $10.3B, with a median of $5.0B. The $6.0B current valuation sits near the midpoint of this range but notably above peer median, suggesting either relative de-risking relative to stage-matched comparables or premium pricing for founder credibility and capital deployment capability. Key uncertainties include comparable company stage heterogeneity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the duration and probability of achieving Series E or exit-relevant inflection points.
